rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code unique among non-null values
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NOT NULL GROUP BY soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per occupation by soc_code (where non-null). After broad code expansion and deduplication, no two rows should share the same soc_code. Duplicate SOC codes would cause fan-out in downstream Gold zone joins and corrupt consumable tables.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Zero duplicate SOC codes after expansion. No detailed SOC code appears in both the direct-match set and the broad-expansion set. No two broad codes expand to overlapping detailed codes.' Physical model: 'Natural key: soc_code (unique among non-null values).' Threshold: 0 duplicates predicted in ~412 rows.

rules.name: SOC code format: XX-XXXX where non-null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NOT NULL AND NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: All non-null SOC codes must match the XX-XXXX format (two digits, hyphen, four digits). After Silver transformation, broad codes (XX-XXX0) should have been expanded to detailed codes (XX-XXXX) or kept as-is if they match BLS exactly. The 6 unmatched broad codes (XX-X000 pattern) will have soc_resolved_method='unresolved' but still pass this regex since XX-X000 matches the XX-XXXX pattern.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'All 290 Bronze SOC codes pass format check. Format: All will be XX-XXXX after normalization. 6 unmatched broad codes will remain as XX-XXX0 or XX-X000.' Physical model: 'CHECK (soc_code IS NULL OR soc_code ~ ^\d{2}-\d{4}$).' All broad/detailed codes match the XX-XXXX regex pattern.

rules.name: Exposure score range: 0-10
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score < 0 OR exposure_score > 10
rules.description: Exposure score is a passthrough from Bronze. Must remain in the 0-10 integer range defined by Karpathy's rubric. The effective range is 1-10 (no zeros observed), but the rubric permits 0, so the DQ rule allows it. Any out-of-range value indicates a data mutation during Silver transformation.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Exposure scores are unchanged from Bronze. Range 1-10, mean 5.31, median 5.0. No mutation risk since Silver is a passthrough.' Physical model: 'CHECK (exposure_score >= 0 AND exposure_score <= 10).' Domain context: 'Score 0 absent. DQ rule range should remain 0-10.' Threshold: 0 violations in 342 Bronze rows; expanded rows inherit valid scores.

rules.name: Exposure score completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score IS NULL
rules.description: exposure_score is NOT NULL in the physical model. Every row -- whether direct, expanded, title-matched, or unresolved -- must carry a score. Expanded rows inherit the broad code's score; unresolved rows retain their Bronze score.
rules.evidence: EDA: '0 null exposure scores in 342 Bronze rows. Score is passthrough -- expanded rows inherit the broad code score.' Physical model: 'exposure_score INTEGER NOT NULL.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: soc_resolved_method enum validity
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_resolved_method NOT IN ('direct', 'title_match', 'broad_expansion', 'unresolved')
rules.description: soc_resolved_method must be one of exactly four valid values: 'direct', 'title_match', 'broad_expansion', 'unresolved'. Any other value indicates a bug in the SOC resolution logic.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Predicted distribution: direct ~59%, broad_expansion ~27%, title_match ~7%, unresolved ~7%.' Physical model: 'CHECK (soc_resolved_method IN (direct, title_match, broad_expansion, unresolved)).' Threshold: 0 violations -- all rows must use exactly one of the four enum values.

rules.name: BLS match rate >= 90% among non-null SOC rows
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN bls_match = true THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(CAST(COUNT(*) AS DOUBLE), 0)) < 0.90 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NOT NULL
rules.description: At least 90% of rows with non-null soc_code must have bls_match = true (meaning the SOC code was found in base.bls_ooh). If less than 90% match, the SOC vintage is wrong or the BLS OOH data is incomplete, and investigation is required before Gold promotion.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'BLS match rate prediction: ~98% of non-null SOC rows. 354 BLS-matched rows out of ~360 non-null SOC rows. Conservative estimate: 90-95% bls_match = true among non-null SOC rows.' Physical model: 'bls_match >= 90% true (non-null SOC).' Threshold: 90% is well below the predicted 98%, providing margin for edge cases.

rules.name: Slug completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE slug IS NULL
rules.description: slug is NOT NULL in the physical model. Every row must retain its Karpathy slug for provenance and traceability. For broad_expansion rows, the original slug of the broad-code row is carried to all expanded detailed-code rows.
rules.evidence: EDA: '0 null slugs in Bronze (100% coverage). Slug is always present (carried from Bronze).' Physical model: 'slug VARCHAR NOT NULL.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: record_id uniqueness and completeness
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: record_id is the surrogate primary key. It must be non-null and unique across all rows. It is a deterministic SHA-256 hash (prefix 'kai') computed from soc_code (when non-null) or slug (when soc_code is null). Duplicates would indicate a grain collision in the hash function.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Deterministic hash, guaranteed unique by construction.' Physical model: 'record_id VARCHAR NOT NULL, PRIMARY KEY (record_id).' Threshold: 0 violations -- hash collisions are theoretically near-impossible with SHA-256 truncated to 16 hex chars on < 500 rows.

rules.name: Row count within expected range: 380-500
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 380 OR cnt > 500 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure)
rules.description: After broad code expansion (342 Bronze rows + ~110 expansion rows - dedup + ~28 title matches), the expected row count is approximately 412. The range 380-500 provides margin for variation in title matching outcomes and expansion counts. Below 380 suggests expansion logic failed; above 500 suggests uncontrolled 1:N expansion.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Predicted Silver Record Count: ~406-412. Recommend tightening to 380-500.' EDA breakdown: '240 direct + 4 broad-to-broad + 110 expanded + ~28 title_match + ~24 unresolved = ~406. Title match 1:N could add more rows.' Physical model: 'Expected row count: ~500+ (342 Bronze rows after broad code expansion and deduplication).' EDA recommends 380-500 as tighter than model's 400-700.

rules.name: Rationale minimum length >= 250 characters
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE LENGTH(rationale) < 250
rules.description: Rationale must be at least 250 characters long per the physical model CHECK constraint. This ensures the LLM-generated explanation is substantive enough to be meaningful for the Fight AI boss narrative display. The shortest observed rationale is 297 chars (47 chars of headroom).
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Rationale lengths all pass the physical model >= 250 char CHECK. Minimum is 297 chars, maximum is 587 chars. The 250-char threshold has 47 chars of headroom above the shortest observed value.' Physical model: 'CHECK (LENGTH(rationale) >= 250).' Threshold: 0 violations in 342 Bronze rows; expanded rows duplicate rationale verbatim.

rules.name: soc_resolved_method distribution: direct >= 50%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN soc_resolved_method = 'direct' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(CAST(COUNT(*) AS DOUBLE), 0)) < 0.50 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure
rules.description: The 'direct' resolution method should account for the majority of rows. EDA predicts ~59% direct (244 of ~412). A drop below 50% would indicate the SOC resolution logic is misclassifying direct matches or the broad expansion is producing disproportionately many rows.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Predicted distribution: direct ~244 (59%). Includes 240 detailed + 4 broad-to-broad exact matches.' Spec originally predicted ~70% direct but EDA corrected to ~59%. Threshold set at 50% (below predicted 59%) to allow margin while still catching gross miscategorization.

rules.name: soc_resolved_method distribution: unresolved <= 15%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(SUM(CASE WHEN soc_resolved_method = 'unresolved' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS DOUBLE) / NULLIF(CAST(COUNT(*) AS DOUBLE), 0)) > 0.15 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure
rules.description: Unresolved rows (no SOC code after all resolution attempts) should not exceed 15% of total rows. EDA predicts ~7% unresolved (~30 of ~412). Exceeding 15% would indicate title matching and broad expansion are both failing at higher-than-expected rates.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Predicted unresolved: ~30 rows (7%) -- includes ~24 null SOC + 6 unmatched broad codes.' Threshold set at 15% (double the predicted rate) to allow margin while still catching systemic resolution failures.

rules.name: Null SOC implies soc_resolved_method = unresolved
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NULL AND soc_resolved_method != 'unresolved'
rules.description: Rows with null soc_code must have soc_resolved_method = 'unresolved'. Any other value on a null-SOC row indicates a bug in the resolution logic (e.g., a title_match that didn't actually populate the soc_code).
rules.evidence: Physical model derivation rules: 'If soc_code is still null after all resolution: unresolved.' This is a logical consistency constraint between two derived fields. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Non-null SOC implies soc_resolved_method != unresolved (except unmatched broad codes)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NOT NULL AND soc_resolved_method = 'unresolved' AND bls_match = true
rules.description: Rows with non-null soc_code and bls_match = true should NOT have soc_resolved_method = 'unresolved'. Note: 6 rows will have non-null SOC + unresolved + bls_match=false (unmatched broad codes like XX-X000). Those are acceptable. But a row that matched BLS should never be classified as unresolved.
rules.evidence: EDA: '6 broad codes (19-5000, 37-3000, 39-2000, 41-4000, 43-6000, 53-5000) have no BLS match -- these carry forward with bls_match=false and soc_resolved_method=unresolved.' These are the only valid case of non-null SOC + unresolved. If bls_match is also true, something is wrong.

rules.name: bls_match = false when soc_code IS NULL
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_code IS NULL AND bls_match = true
rules.description: Rows with null soc_code must have bls_match = false. You cannot have a BLS match without a SOC code to match on.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'All null SOC rows are bls_match = false by definition.' Physical model: 'bls_match: False for null soc_code rows.' Threshold: 0 violations -- logical impossibility.

rules.name: occupation_title completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE occupation_title IS NULL
rules.description: occupation_title is NOT NULL in the physical model. Every row must have a title, whether direct from Bronze or inherited during expansion.
rules.evidence: EDA: '0 null occupation_titles in 342 Bronze rows.' Physical model: 'occupation_title VARCHAR NOT NULL.' Expanded rows inherit the title from the broad-code row. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: category completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE category IS NULL
rules.description: category is NOT NULL in the physical model. Every row must be assigned to one of Karpathy's 25 BLS category groups.
rules.evidence: EDA: '0 null categories in 342 Bronze rows. 25 distinct values.' Physical model: 'category VARCHAR NOT NULL.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: source_load_date completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE source_load_date IS NULL
rules.description: source_load_date is NOT NULL in the physical model. Pipeline metadata field renamed from Bronze load_date.
rules.evidence: Physical model: 'source_load_date DATE NOT NULL.' All Bronze rows have non-null load_date. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: ingested_at completeness: 0% null
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE ingested_at IS NULL
rules.description: ingested_at is NOT NULL in the physical model. Silver zone write timestamp generated at transformation time.
rules.evidence: Physical model: 'ingested_at TIMESTAMP NOT NULL. Generated at transformation time via datetime.now().' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: record_id format: kai- prefix with 16 hex chars
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE NOT regexp_matches(record_id, '^kai-[0-9a-f]{16}$')
rules.description: record_id must follow the format 'kai-' followed by exactly 16 lowercase hexadecimal characters. This is the deterministic SHA-256 truncated hash produced by compute_grain_id().
rules.evidence: Physical model: 'Deterministic surrogate key: compute_grain_id(row, [soc_code], prefix=kai). Format: kai-<16 hex chars>.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Exposure score is integer (no fractional values)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE exposure_score != CAST(exposure_score AS INT)
rules.description: exposure_score must be an integer. Fractional values would indicate a type coercion error during Silver transformation. Score is a passthrough from Bronze where it is already validated as integer.
rules.evidence: EDA: 'Type: INTEGER. Score is passthrough.' Bronze DQ rule RAW-KAI-018 validates integer type at ingestion. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Referential integrity: bls_match=true implies soc_code exists in base.bls_ooh
rules.sql: SELECT k.* FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure k LEFT JOIN base.bls_ooh b ON k.soc_code = b.soc_code WHERE k.bls_match = true AND b.soc_code IS NULL
rules.description: If bls_match is true, the soc_code must actually exist in base.bls_ooh. A true bls_match with no corresponding BLS record indicates the bls_match flag was set incorrectly during transformation.
rules.evidence: Physical model derivation: 'bls_match = soc_code IN (SELECT soc_code FROM base.bls_ooh).' This rule cross-validates the derived flag against the actual BLS reference table. Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: broad_expansion rows must have bls_match = true
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.karpathy_ai_exposure WHERE soc_resolved_method = 'broad_expansion' AND bls_match = false
rules.description: Rows created by broad code expansion are by definition matched to BLS detailed codes. If a broad_expansion row has bls_match = false, the expansion logic created a row for a SOC code that doesn't exist in BLS, which is a bug.
rules.evidence: Physical model algorithm: 'For each detailed code found [in base.bls_ooh]: create a new row with bls_match = true (by definition, since we found it in BLS).' EDA: '110 expanded rows, all from BLS-verified detailed codes.' Threshold: 0 violations.
